questions.question: What is the general name for problems that seek to maximize or minimize a specific quantity like profit or cost?
questions.explanation: Problems which seek to maximise or minimise profit or cost form a general class of problems called optimisation problems.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The linear function Z = ax + by, which is to be maximized or minimized, is called the:
questions.explanation: Linear function Z = ax + by, where a, b are constants, which has to be maximised or minimised is called a linear objective function.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The variables x and y in an LPP are often referred to as:
questions.explanation: In an LPP, the variables x and y are called decision variables.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Linear inequalities or restrictions on the variables of a linear programming problem are called:
questions.explanation: The linear inequalities or equations or restrictions on the variables of a linear programming problem are called linear constraints.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The common region determined by all the constraints including non-negative constraints is called the:
questions.explanation: The common region determined by all the constraints including non-negative constraints x, y ≥ 0 of a linear programming problem is called the feasible region.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Any point in the feasible region that gives the optimal value (maximum or minimum) of the objective function is called an:
questions.explanation: Any point in the feasible region that gives the optimal value (maximum or minimum) of the objective function is called an optimal solution.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The 'Corner Point Method' is based on the theorem that the optimal value occurs at:
options: The vertices (corner points) of the feasible region
questions.explanation: The optimal value must occur at a corner point (vertex) of the feasible region.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If the feasible region is ________, the objective function Z may or may not have a maximum or minimum value.
questions.explanation: When the feasible region is unbounded, a maximum or a minimum value of the objective function may not exist.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If two corner points of a feasible region produce the same maximum value of Z, then:
options: Only those two points are solutions
Every point on the line segment joining these two points is also an optimal solution
questions.explanation: If two corner points of the feasible region are both optimal solutions of the same type, then every point on the line segment joining them is also an optimal solution.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which mathematician suggested the efficient 'Simplex Method' in 1947?
questions.explanation: In 1947, the American economist, G. B. Dantzig suggested an efficient method known as the simplex method.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The first formulated linear programming problem in 1941 was the:
questions.explanation: The first problem formulated in 1941 was the well known transportation problem.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Who formulated the transportation problem independently in 1941?
questions.explanation: The transportation problem was formulated in 1941 by the Russian mathematician L. Kantorovich and the American economist F. L. Hitchcock independently.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In which year were L. Kantorovich and T. C. Koopmans awarded the Nobel Prize in Economics for their work on LPP?
questions.explanation: L. Kantorovich and T. C. Koopmans were awarded the nobel prize in the year 1975 in economics.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Linear programming problems developed significantly during which major global event?
questions.explanation: In the World War II, when war operations had to be planned to economise expenditure... linear programming problems came to the forefront.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A special class of LPP where the objective is to determine the number of different products to produce to maximize profit is:
questions.explanation: Manufacturing problems involve determining the number of units of different products which should be produced and sold by a firm... to maximise profit.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The problem of determining the amount of different types of nutrients which should be included in a diet to minimize cost is a:
questions.explanation: Diet problems involve determining the amount of constituents which should be included... so as to minimise the cost.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Non-negative constraints in a typical LPP (involving x and y) are expressed as:
questions.explanation: Non-negative constraints are typically x ≥ 0 and y ≥ 0.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The objective function in a linear programming problem must be a:
questions.explanation: In LPP, both the objective function and the constraints must be linear.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In a graphical solution of an LPP, the feasible region is always a:
questions.explanation: The feasible region for an LPP is a convex polygon.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Any point outside the feasible region is called an:
questions.explanation: Any point outside the feasible region is called an infeasible solution.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The optimal value of the objective function is attained at a corner point. This is stated in:
questions.explanation: Theorem 1 states that the optimal value (if it exists) will occur at a corner point.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In the furniture dealer example, the dealer has Rs 50,000 and can store 60 pieces. These conditions are called:
questions.explanation: Investment and storage capacity limits are linear constraints in the problem.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If the feasible region is bounded, then the objective function Z = ax + by has:
options: Both a maximum and a minimum value
questions.explanation: Theorem 2 states if R is bounded, Z has both a maximum and a minimum value on R.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which type of problem determines the cheapest way to ship products from plants to markets?
questions.explanation: Transportation problems determine a schedule to transport a product from plants to different markets... so that the total transportation cost is minimum.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A polygonal region is called 'bounded' if:
options: It can be enclosed within a circle
It is located in the third quadrant
questions.explanation: A region is bounded if it can be enclosed in a circle.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for a problem to be a Linear Programming Problem?
options: The feasible region must be circular
questions.explanation: An LPP requires linear objectives, linear constraints, and non-negative variables, but the region is polygonal, not circular.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: What is the first step in the Corner Point Method?
options: Calculate the value of Z at corner points
Find the feasible region and its corner points
questions.explanation: Step 1 is to find the feasible region and its corner points.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If Z = ax + by has a maximum value M in an unbounded feasible region, we must check if:
options: ax + by > M has common points with the feasible region
ax + by < M has common points
questions.explanation: In an unbounded region, M is the maximum if the open half plane ax + by > M has no common point with the feasible region.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which mathematician is associated with the quote 'The mathematical experience of the student is incomplete if he never had the opportunity to solve a problem invented by himself'?
questions.explanation: The chapter opens with this quote from G. Polya.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In the simplex method, 'simplex' refers to an ________ procedure.
questions.explanation: The simplex method is described as an iterative procedure to solve LPP.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Decision variables in LPP are always:
questions.explanation: In LPP, decision variables x and y are always non-negative (x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The term 'Linear' in LPP means that the relationship between variables is of the ________ degree.
questions.explanation: Linear implies that variables appear only with the first power.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Who described the LPP problem of determining an optimal diet in 1945?
questions.explanation: In 1945, an English economist, G. Stigler, described the optimal diet problem.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A corner point of a feasible region is a point in the region which is the ________ of two boundary lines.
questions.explanation: A corner point is the intersection of two boundary lines of the region.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If the constraints in an LPP are inconsistent, the feasible region is:
questions.explanation: If there is no region that satisfies all constraints, there is no feasible region.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Feasible solutions are ________ which satisfy all given constraints.
questions.explanation: Points within or on the boundary of the feasible region represent feasible solutions.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In the furniture problem, the objective function to maximize is profit Z =:
questions.explanation: With profit of 250 per table and 75 per chair, Z = 250x + 75y.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If the objective function Z = ax + by has a minimum value m in an unbounded region, we test the half-plane:
questions.explanation: m is the minimum if ax + by < m has no points in common with the feasible region.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Graphical method for solving LPP is generally used for problems with how many variables?
questions.explanation: Graphical methods are used for LPP with two variables.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: What happens if a maximum value is required but the feasible region ax + by > M has points in common with the unbounded region?
questions.explanation: If there are common points, Z has no maximum value in that region.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which of the following describes the 'Bounded' property of a region?
options: It has at least one corner
It can be enclosed in a circle
It is only in the first quadrant
It is made of straight lines
questions.explanation: A region is bounded if it can be enclosed in a circle.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The feasible solution that gives the 'Best' value of the objective is the ________ solution.
questions.explanation: The 'optimal' solution is the best one (max or min) among all feasible ones.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: L. Kantorovich, a key figure in LPP, was of which nationality?
questions.explanation: L. Kantorovich was a Russian mathematician.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In 1941, Hitchcock's nationality was:
questions.explanation: F. L. Hitchcock was an American economist.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Linear constraints in LPP are represented graphically by:
questions.explanation: Linear equations/inequalities are represented by straight lines on a graph.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The Corner Point Method works because of the ________ of the feasible region.
questions.explanation: The optimal value occurs at the extreme points (vertices) of the convex feasible region.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The total number of constituents included in the optimal diet problem are meant to satisfy minimum ________ requirements.
questions.explanation: Diet problems focus on meeting specific nutrient requirements at minimum cost.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If an LPP has multiple optimal solutions, they must occur ________.
options: Along a boundary line segment joining two corner points
questions.explanation: Multiple optimal solutions occur along the edge joining two optimal corner points.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Modern computers allow the application of LPP to ________ problems.
questions.explanation: Computers make it possible to apply LPP to increasingly complex real-world problems.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Constraints of the type x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0 are specifically called:
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names x, y ≥ 0 as non-negative constraints.
questions.subject: Mathematics
